findings.issue: The opening frame is a transition story ('Backend Transition Profile' / 'candidate transitioning from analytics') instead of a direct role + domain anchor, so the first-screen signal is weaker than it should be for a senior backend/networking role.
findings.fix: Lead with 'Backend/Security SWE' or 'Backend engineer focused on security and Linux-based services,' then mention analytics as prior background in a secondary clause.

findings.issue: This internship section reads like breadth signaling across 4 languages (Go, Java, C++, Python) plus several infra tools (Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, GitHub Actions, pytest). For an intern, that density looks like stack bloat unless the ownership boundary is tightened.
findings.fix: Collapse the section into 2 core stacks: one gRPC/backend stack and one C++/Python validation stack. Keep Docker/Kubernetes/GitHub Actions/AWS as support tooling in a single bullet and explicitly mark the workflow as team-maintained/internal.

revision_priority: [MUST] Narrow the TikTok internship to 2 core stacks and make support infra explicitly auxiliary.
[SHOULD] Rewrite the opening Summary to lead with backend/security rather than transition language.
[SHOULD] Add one Tier-1 scope metric near the top of TikTok or DiDi if available.
[NICE] Keep the rest of the content; it already covers the Cisco must-haves.
raw_json.revision_instructions: [MUST] Rework the TikTok internship so it reads as one coherent backend/service stack plus one validation stack, not a broad multi-language/multi-infra spread. Keep the technologies, but attach each to one concrete ownership line and make Docker/Kubernetes/AWS/GitHub Actions clearly support tooling in a team-maintained/internal workflow. [SHOULD] Rewrite the first Summary sentence to lead with backend/security direction first, then mention the analytics transition second. [SHOULD] If possible, add one Tier-1 scope signal near the top of the resume (service volume, event volume, team size, or release cadence) so the first impression is not driven mostly by time-reduction percentages.
